name: sigma70_promoter_tuning
description: Bacterial expression cassette with a *variable* sigma70 promoter upstream of a fixed reporter context capped by a fixed rho-independent terminator. The variable 80 bp promoter is scored by the `sigma70-promoter` constraint against the canonical E. coli consensus (-35 box `TTGACA`, -10 box `TATAAT`, spacer 17 bp; Hawley & McClure 1983), and by `gc-content` and `max-homopolymer` to keep the upstream sequence within a synthesis-friendly composition window. Downstream of the promoter four pieces are held fixed: a Shine-Dalgarno ribosome-binding site followed by a KpnI cloning site (5'-AGGAGAAAGGTACC-3'; SD core AGGAGA pairs with the anti-SD region of E. coli 16S rRNA), a full E. coli codon-optimized eGFP coding sequence carrying its own ATG start codon and a C-terminal 6xHis tag, a TAA stop, and a fixed 44 bp rho-independent intrinsic terminator (GC-rich stem + U-rich 3' tract). Swap the eGFP CDS for your own gene to retarget, or swap the SD for another RBS to tune expression. This is a focused teaching and toy example for single-region promoter tuning around a fixed reporter and terminator; for a richer multi-construct multi-segment template see `multiple_segments.json`.

segments.label: Variable sigma70 promoter region (-35 / spacer / -10 / discriminator)
Fixed rho-independent terminator (GC-rich stem + U-rich 3' tract, 44 bp)
